title: Credit Card
description: One hand-curated credit card. Rates are in earn-units per dollar: for cash-back cards a rate of 2 means 2% cash back; for points cards a rate of 3 means 3 points per dollar, converted to USD via data/meta/point-valuations.yaml. Membership of category/merchant/program strings in the meta registries is enforced by scripts/validate_cards.py, not by this schema.

id.description: Stable slug, must match the filename (without .yaml)

issuer.description: Issuer slug, must match the parent directory name

program.description: Key into data/meta/point-valuations.yaml (use 'cash' for cash-back cards)

base_rate.description: Catch-all earn rate applied to spend not matched by any category or merchant reward. For conditionally boosted base rates (Apple Card 2% via Apple Pay vs 1% physical) store the unconditional baseline here and the boost in base_rate_conditional

portal.description: statement-descriptors.yaml key naming the issuer portal this card's portal_only rates book through. Required by the validator when any category reward has portal_only: true. Portal use is assumed by the optimizer (a user books through the portal of whichever card they hold), so this is not a questionnaire item — it names the portal in the discounted-rate note and lets statement import categorize portal purchases. Card-level (not issuer-level) because e.g. Citi AAdvantage cards use aadvantage_hotels, not citi_travel

unlocks_transfers.description: true = holding this card unlocks its currency's transfer partners for every card of the same program in the portfolio (Sapphire for Freedom-family UR, Strata Premier/Elite for TYP, Autograph Journey for WF Rewards). Only meaningful on programs marked transfer_gateway_required in point-valuations.yaml (validator-enforced); without a gateway in the scored portfolio those currencies are valued at floor_cpp even in optimistic mode

required_membership.description: Paid membership/subscription that is a prerequisite for holding the card or earning its rewards (Sam's Club membership, Amazon Prime, REI Co-op, Robinhood Gold). Unscored by default — the optimizer assumes the user already holds it — unless card_exclusive is true, in which case annual_cost_usd is scored as an ongoing cost. Omit for cards without one; free credit-union membership goes in approval.notes instead

annual_cost_usd.description: Current annual cost of the cheapest qualifying tier

card_exclusive.description: True when the membership has no material standalone value apart from the card (Robinhood Gold): the optimizer subtracts annual_cost_usd from ongoing and year-1 net. Omit/false for memberships people hold anyway (Costco, Prime, Sam's Club)

note.description: What the membership gates (card eligibility, earn rate, redemption) and which tier qualifies

max_annual_rewards_usd.description: Card-wide cap on total reward value earnable per year, in USD (Sam's Cash $5,000/calendar year). Optimizer clamps the card's spend earnings at this value. Omit when uncapped

kind.description: statement_credit (default) = cash-equivalent credit valued at face. in_kind = non-cash recurring benefit (hotel free night, companion certificate, lounge passes) whose amount_usd is a curator-estimated value — the optimizer always applies a capture haircut, and the estimation reasoning belongs in notes

amount_usd.description: Face value per period (for kind: in_kind, the curator-estimated USD value per period)

amount_points.description: Points/miles granted per period instead of USD (anniversary bonus points, spend-triggered point drops). Points cards only — valued via the card's currency.program

category.description: Optional canonical category this credit offsets

usage_keys.description: Usage-question item keys (data/meta/usage-questions.yaml), anyOf semantics: the optimizer values this credit at $0 unless the user confirmed at least one of these keys in user.confirmed_usage. Use for merchant/service/brand-locked credits (DoorDash, Resy, Delta Stays, free-night certs). Membership in the registry is enforced by scripts/validate_cards.py

automatic.description: true = the credit pays out with no merchant-specific action by the cardholder (anniversary bonus points, anniversary cash credits) — valued at full face, never usage-gated. Mutually exclusive with usage_keys and category (validator-enforced). Every credit must carry at least one of category / usage_keys / automatic

unlock_spend_usd.description: Card spend per period required before this credit pays out (e.g. a $200 flight credit after $10,000 in a calendar year). Omit for unconditional credits

requires_enrollment.description: true when the benefit must be enrolled/activated before it pays out — a major capture-rate drag worth recording structurally

portal_only.description: true = redeemable only by booking through an issuer travel/hotel portal (Amex Travel/FHR, Chase Travel/The Edit, Capital One Travel, Citi Travel, Delta Stays, United Hotels). The optimizer counts at most ONE portal credit per portfolio (a user books through a single portal), so overlapping portal credits across stacked cards don't inflate portfolio net value. Per-card display is unaffected. Distinct from any-travel statement credits and airline-incidental credits, which are NOT portal_only

expires.description: Last day of a promotional credit with an announced end date (e.g. 'offer ends 12/31/2026'). Omit for evergreen credits. The validator flags credits past this date

auto_expire.description: true marks a scraper-added, self-expiring promotional credit that the daily expirer may safely DELETE once past its `expires` date (requires `expires` — validator-enforced). Omit/false for curator-added credits, which are only reported, never auto-deleted

realistic_capture_rate_note.description: Honest note on how much of this credit a typical user actually captures

signup_bonus.description: The card's permanent/standard signup bonus; null when the card has no current public signup bonus. A limited-time ELEVATED offer above this goes in the sibling signup_bonus_limited_time — the optimizer values whichever is active as-of the date and auto-reverts here when the elevated offer expires

signup_bonus_limited_time.description: A limited-time elevated signup offer above the permanent signup_bonus (dual-bonus model). Same shape as signup_bonus but ALWAYS carries an end date (`expires` required). The optimizer scores this while live-and-feasible and auto-reverts to signup_bonus once past `expires`. Record the standard offer in signup_bonus (or its notes) so the card still shows a bonus after this expires. Omit for cards with no elevated offer

approval.description: Estimated credit profile needed for likely approval. Issuers don't publish cutoffs — these are curator estimates; cite reasoning/sources in notes. The optimizer filters recommendations by the user's stated credit standing.

credit_tier.description: building = secured/credit-builder, approves thin or damaged files; fair ~580+; good ~670+; very_good ~740+; excellent ~800+ or premium-card underwriting

estimated_min_score.description: Optional finer-grained FICO estimate for likely approval

notes.description: Issuer-specific approval rules worth knowing (e.g. Chase 5/24, Amex once-per-lifetime bonus, deposit required)

closed_loop.description: Present only for closed-loop store cards. The card is usable ONLY at the listed merchants; the optimizer may assign only those merchants' spend to it, and base_rate applies within that restriction.

relationship_boost.description: Earn boost conditional on a banking relationship with the issuer (BofA Preferred Rewards 25-75%, U.S. Bank Smartly balance tiers). Recorded but NOT scored in v1 — the optimizer models the no-relationship baseline. Omit entirely for cards without one.

program.description: Issuer program name, e.g. 'Bank of America Preferred Rewards'

min_balance_usd.description: Qualifying combined deposit/investment balance for this tier

requirement.description: Non-balance qualifying condition when the tier isn't balance-keyed (SoFi 'qualifying direct deposit each 30-day period', Truist 'Marquee Checking account'). Provide this and/or min_balance_usd

boost_pct.description: Percentage increase applied to rewards earnings at this tier, e.g. 75 = 75% more

note.description: Mechanics and caveats: which accounts qualify, caps on boosted spend, whether the boost applies to base or bonus earn

benefit_flags.description: Binary attributes (e.g. primary_rental_insurance) — not scored in v1

sources.description: Exact links every fact in this file came from — pasted by whoever (human or AI) put the fact in, at the moment they put it in. Each entry declares which blocks it supports so a validator or reviewer can trace any number back to a URL.

supports.description: Which schema blocks this link backs

accessed.description: When the link was actually read

added_by.description: Human name/handle, or the AI model that pasted it

note.description: Pinpoint within the source, e.g. 'rates table, footnote 3' or 'terms PDF p.2'

confidence.description: high = checked against issuer terms; medium = checked against a reputable secondary source; low = drafted from memory, needs human verification

fallback_rate.description: Earn rate on spend above the cap

shared_cap_id.description: Marks caps that draw from ONE combined spend pool across multiple reward entries on this card (e.g. '2x on gas + groceries, combined, on the first $5,000/yr'). Every entry carrying the same id must state identical period and max_spend_usd (each states the full pool, not a share); the optimizer treats them as a single pool. Omit for independent caps

bonusValue.description: At least one of points / usd; both together for mixed bonuses (e.g. 100,000 points + $100 statement credit)

signupOffer.description: One signup-bonus offer body, shared by the permanent signup_bonus and the elevated signup_bonus_limited_time. `expires` lives here (not the wrapper) so signup_bonus_limited_time's allOf+required[expires] survives additionalProperties:false.

tiers.description: Additional bonus tranches on top of the base value, each unlocked at a higher CUMULATIVE spend within the same window_months (e.g. '70k after $3k, +20k more after $5k total' → value 70k points / spend_requirement_usd 3000, plus one tier of 20k points at spend_requirement_usd 5000). Tier spend requirements must exceed the base requirement and ascend

first_year_match.description: Discover-style Cashback Match: the issuer matches all rewards earned in year one, dollar-for-dollar. When true, omit value/spend_requirement_usd/window_months/tiers — the optimizer values the match as the card's own computed first-year earnings

expires.description: Last day the offer is available, when the issuer announces one. Optional on the permanent signup_bonus (omit for evergreen offers); REQUIRED on signup_bonus_limited_time. The validator flags offers past this date so they get re-checked.

choice.description: Only for choose-your-own-category rewards (Citi Custom Cash, BofA Customized Cash), used with the 'choice' pseudo-category. The optimizer expands the card into one variant per option and lets the portfolio search pick the best configuration per combination.

options.description: Categories the cardholder may select for this reward

note.description: How selection works (user-picked vs automatic top category, change cadence)

earnRatio.description: Variable earn on an explicit_only category (Bilt housing) whose points-per-dollar multiplier is a step function of a spend RATIO = (everyday spend charged to this card) / (this category's spend on this card), per billing cycle. Bilt's 'Everyday Spend Ratio': 0x below 25%, 0.5x at 25-49%, 0.75x at 50-74%, 1x at 75-99%, 1.25x at 100%+, with a per-cycle points floor. The optimizer resolves the multiplier in a post-assignment steering pass (scripts/optimize.py) and re-prices the reward line; the reward's top-level `rate` is the max tier rate, used only for line ordering and dominance pruning.

denominator_category.description: The category whose per-card spend is the ratio denominator (e.g. 'housing'). Must be a non-pseudo category and normally this reward's own category.

floor_points_per_cycle.description: Minimum points earned per billing cycle on the category regardless of ratio (Bilt: 250).

cycles_per_year.description: Billing cycles per year (12) — annualizes the per-cycle floor.

tiers.description: Ratio tiers ascending by min_ratio; first entry must have min_ratio 0. The applied rate is the highest tier whose min_ratio <= ratio.

min_ratio.description: Inclusive lower bound of the everyday/denominator ratio for this tier (0, 0.25, 0.5, ...).

rate.description: Points per dollar earned on the category while in this tier.

portal_only.description: true if the rate only applies when booked through the issuer's portal

requires_enrollment.description: true when the rate pays nothing unless the cardholder actively enrolls/activates the category each cycle (Elan Max Cash Preferred category enrollment)

expires.description: Last day a LIMITED-TIME elevated earn rate applies (e.g. a promotional 5x through a stated date). Omit for evergreen rates. The optimizer zeros this row past the date (spend falls through to base); the validator flags rows past it

auto_expire.description: true marks a scraper-added, self-expiring promotional earn row that the daily expirer may safely DELETE once past its `expires` date (requires `expires` — validator-enforced). Omit/false for curator-added rows

expires.description: Last day a LIMITED-TIME elevated earn rate applies. Omit for evergreen rates. The optimizer zeros this row past the date (spend falls through to base); the validator flags rows past it

auto_expire.description: true marks a scraper-added, self-expiring promotional earn row that the daily expirer may safely DELETE once past its `expires` date (requires `expires` — validator-enforced). Omit/false for curator-added rows

conditionalRate.description: A higher earn rate gated on a condition the schema can't model structurally: a paid membership tier (Walmart+ 5% vs 3%, Sam's Club Plus 3% vs 1%, My Best Buy Total), a payment method (Apple Pay 2% vs 1% physical), or an account/status relationship (PenFed Honors Advantage). The sibling rate MUST be the unconditional baseline — recorded but NOT scored in v1; the optimizer models the baseline

rate.description: The boosted rate when the condition holds. Must exceed the baseline rate

requires.description: The condition, stated plainly: 'linked Walmart+ membership', 'paid via Apple Pay', 'Sam's Club Plus tier'
